| spouse = 
| children = 
| notable_works = 
}}

'''Hiko-Sashima no Mikoto''' (彦狭島命, dates of birth and death unknown) was a nobleman during the {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}. In the ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'', he is also referred to as '''Hiko-Samema no Mikoto''' (日子寤間命).

== Overview ==
The ''{{ill|Kojiki|en|Kojiki|qid=Q813031|lt=Kojiki|1=Kojiki}}'' and ''{{ill|Nihon Shoki|en|Nihon Shoki|qid=Q813935|lt=Nihon Shoki|1=Nihon Shoki}}'' describe Hiko-Sashima no Mikoto as a son of {{ill|Emperor Kōrei|en|Emperor Kōrei|qid=Q313041|lt=Emperor Kōrei|1=Emperor Kōrei}}, born to his consort Wajinomi no Woto. He is identified as an ancestor of the Ujikao no Omi (針間牛鹿臣) and Umi no Atae clans in these records. {{ill|Itō Hirobumi|en|Itō Hirobumi|qid=Q174971|lt=Hirobumi Itō|1=Itō Hirobumi}}, in the "Genealogy of the Itō Marquisate," claimed descent from Hiko-Sashima through his third son, {{ill|Ochi no Ōji|ja|小千王子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ochi no Ōji|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.

Hiko-Sashima no Mikoto is enshrined as the main deity at {{ill|Iyo Shrine|lt=Iyo Shrine|lt=Iyo Shrine (Ehime)|ja|伊予神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iyo Shrine (Ehime)|qq=}} in {{ill|Masaki, Ehime|en|Masaki, Ehime|qid=Q1350953|lt=Matsumae, Ehime|1=Masaki, Ehime}}, {{ill|Ehime Prefecture|en|Ehime Prefecture|qid=Q123376|lt=Ehime Prefecture|1=Ehime Prefecture}}.
